parent_section: §6.3.9.1 Functional Tests (FT) — FT-Assumptions — QRS Shared Assumptions
verbatim: 1.	The name of a QRS instrument is described under the variable --CAT in the relevant QRS domain (i.e., FT, QS, RS), and may be either abbreviations or longer names. For example, "ADAS-COG", "BPI SHORT FORM", and "APACHE II" are all --CATs which are shortened names for the instruments they represent, whereas "4 STAIR ASCEND" is the FTCAT for the instrument of the same name. Sponsors should always reference CDISC Controlled Terminology.

parent_section: §6.3.9.1 Functional Tests (FT) — FT-Assumptions — QRS Shared Assumptions
verbatim: i.	QRS subtotal, total, etc. scores listed on the CRF are considered captured data and are included in the instrument's controlled terminology.

parent_section: §6.3.9.1 Functional Tests (FT) — FT-Examples — Example 1 — 6-Minute Walk Test (SIX MINUTE WALK)
§6.3.9 Questionnaires, Ratings, and Scales (QRS) Domains (FT, QS, RS)

verbatim: | QSORRESU | Original Units | Char | (UNIT) | Variable Qualifier | Original units in which the data were collected. The unit for QSORRES, such as minutes or seconds or the units associated with a visual analog scale. | Perm |

4. Sponsors should always consult published QRS supplements for guidance on submitting derived information in a SDTMIG QRS domain. Derived variable results in QRS are usually considered captured data. If sponsors operationally derive variable results, then the derived records that are submitted in a QRS domain should be flagged by --DRVFL.

parent_section: §6.3.9 Questionnaires, Ratings, and Scales (QRS) Domains (FT, QS, RS)
§6.3.9.3 Disease Response and Clin Classification (RS) — RS-Specification
verbatim: | RSSTAT | Completion Status | Char | (ND) | Record Qualifier | Used to indicate the response assessment was not performed. Should be null if a result exists in RSORRES. | Perm |
